metrics.narrative: Pain is fully quantified from the 20 Apr 2026 commercial-estimation call: sourcing scope of ~100 tenders/year and 5,000 purchase orders/year, ~4,500 suppliers to migrate, 400+ contracts under management, and roughly $250M base annual spend rising to $400-500M including CAPEX/tenders (Kamil Naseef, Avoma 20 Apr 2026). Zycus's own recommendation of a big-bang, single-country deployment was explicitly tied to reducing implementation cost, time and risk for this scale (Zycus AE, Avoma 20 Apr 2026). No hard ROI/dollar-savings figure has yet been quantified back to the buyer; that remains a gap.

champion.narrative: Kamil Naseef (Advisor to the Procurement & Contracts) was a strong, engaged champion through April-May 2026: he ran a detailed two-hour commercial-estimation session live with the Zycus account team on 20 Apr 2026, filling out usage tables in real time and pushing for optional pricing on 5-year terms, fortnightly refresh, Anna autonomous sourcing, risk management and Merlin intake, and committed to plan the RFP release around the Zycus estimate. What is now missing: since the 8 Jun 2026 delay disclosure, Kamil has not responded to two direct re-engagement attempts (18 Jun and 30 Jun 2026), a 78-day silence versus the last verified two-way contact on 2026-04-20. He has not yet been converted into an internal advocate who pushes Zycus specifically within the tender committee.

competition.narrative: Kamil Naseef named Coupa and GEP Worldwide directly on the 14 Apr 2026 discovery call as the alternatives under evaluation, describing the search as being for 'a Zycus, Coupa, GEP type solution' rather than a large ERP bolt-on. All three are expected to be invited into the same RFP once it issues. No fresh competitive intelligence exists past 14 Apr 2026 because the RFP itself has not yet gone out; the wedge remains Zycus's agentic AI (Merlin autonomous negotiation, Merlin Intake via the relevant stakeholder which Waseef already uses), full native S2P suite versus point solutions, and regional Gulf implementation experience.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is explicit and owner-named: Kamil Naseef described Yardi ERP as having 'very limited S2C functionality,' leaving sourcing and contracting run manually through email and Excel across 100+ tenders and 5,000+ POs a year, no spend visibility/analytics, no contract management system, and no supplier performance tracking (Avoma 14 Apr 2026; SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c Identify_pain__c). He also flagged that the growing ~15-20 person procurement team cannot scale with manual processes (Avoma 14 Apr 2026 key_takeaways). Kamil is the confirmed pain owner.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not been discussed; the deal has not progressed past budgetary estimation and is still awaiting RFP issuance, so MSA/SOW, legal review and procurement channel are all premature. The one concrete paper-adjacent signal is a live compliance blocker: SF MEDDPICC 2.0 flags that Zycus currently only offers UAE/KSA/Germany hosting while Waseef is a government-funded entity that may require in-country Qatar data residency - this needs resolution before contracting can even be scoped. Visibility will come once the RFP is issued and technical/compliance responses are exchanged.

economic_buyer.narrative: No single named economic buyer has been engaged directly by Zycus. Kamil Naseef stated on the 14 Apr 2026 call that 'a tender committee was responsible for making final decisions regarding values that exceeded procurement limits' (Avoma 14 Apr 2026), and SF MEDDPICC 2.0 confirms the Tender Committee has already approved budget to change/add the S2P system, with C-level leadership at Waseef driving the initiative and the parent company ('Baba') also represented on the committee. Budget authority is therefore structurally known but no individual name or direct Zycus contact exists; treated as the CFO or senior management group / Tender committee members.

sources: Avoma call 2026-04-20 (action_item: 'Plan for RFP release by end of April 2026')
decision_process.narrative: The process is RFP-driven and was mapped clearly on 20 Apr 2026: submit QIT inputs, build a budgetary estimate, get feedback, confirm stop/go, then Kamil issues the RFP to Zycus, Coupa and GEP for tender-committee review and final award. Kamil is running the RFP himself with no external consultant. However the process has now stalled at the RFP-issuance step: originally targeted end-April 2026, pushed to 'mid-June' on 8 Jun 2026, and still not issued as of 7 Jul 2026 (3+ weeks past the revised date). The evaluation and committee-approval steps that follow have not yet been reached.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well articulated: a standalone AI-enabled S2P platform (not a bolt-on to the relevant stakeholder, which has 'very limited S2C functionality'), native Yardi integration via a single interface, AI capability across analyze/CLM/invoicing, Arabic reporting option (interface itself can stay English), and 5-year vs 3-year commercial terms compared side by side (Kamil Naseef, Avoma 14 Apr and 20 Apr 2026). SF MEDDPICC 2.0 adds Qatar data-hosting/residency and non-conflict with the existing Yardi Vendor Cafe supplier portal as implied criteria not yet resolved.

flags: SINGLE-THREADED RISK: Zero buyer-side contact roles on opp; entire deal hinges on Kamil Naseef (partner-domain contact, not direct buyer), who has not responded in 67 days and did not reply to 18 Jun owner outreach. If Kamil deprioritizes or leaves, deal is lost. IMMEDIATE ACTION: re-engage Kamil, request intro to CFO and IT head (Kamil mentioned IT head should join next call on 14 Apr), and add buyer-side stakeholders to opp contact roles.

UNMAPPED: CFO or management group holds budget authority (per Kamil 14 Apr) but not identified, not engaged, and no evidence this project has executive sponsorship. The one-month delay (8 Jun, buyer focused on other priorities) signals lack of EB commitment. Without EB validation, this remains a price-check RFP, not a funded project. IMMEDIATE ACTION: ask Kamil to facilitate a brief CFO or senior management intro (frame as 'validate strategic fit and timeline' vs 'sell'), and co-create a business case Kamil can present internally to secure budget/priority.

AI DIFFERENTIATION IS THE WEDGE: Buyer explicitly interested in AI (Merlin intake, agentic Anna, contract AI review) and Kamil expressed enthusiasm ('I liked it, actually'). GEP and Coupa cannot match Zycus AI depth at this deal size. LEVERAGE THIS: make AI the centerpiece of the RFP response and demo (show Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, Anna on tail spend, contract AI review), quantify the efficiency gain (e.g. X hours saved per tender, Y% faster contract review), and position Zycus as the AI-first platform vs. GEP's consulting-led model and Coupa's legacy multi-product complexity. This is the highest-leverage play to justify any price premium and win the technical evaluation.

AI DIFFERENTIATION IS THE WEDGE: Buyer explicitly interested in AI (Merlin intake, agentic Anna, contract AI review) and Kamil expressed enthusiasm ('I liked it, actually'). GEP and Coupa cannot match Zycus AI depth at this deal size. LEVERAGE THIS: make AI the centerpiece of the RFP response and demo (show Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, Anna on tail spend, contract AI review), quantify the efficiency gain (e.g. X hours saved per tender, Y% faster contract review), and position Zycus as the AI-first platform vs. GEP's consulting-led model and Coupa's legacy multi-product complexity. This is the highest-leverage play to justify any price premium and win the technical evaluation.
